summary:
Early discharge may reduce adherence to the optimal 24–72 hour blood draw window for neonatal metabolic screening, but nurse home visits support adequate coverage by enabling ongoing parent education, structured follow-up, and alternative testing methods such as scheduled dried blood spot collections. This approach helps maintain screening effectiveness—even when initial collection happens outside standard timelines—for specific metabolic conditions.  

Final Answer: \boxed{C}
